channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇹🇭 Train crash kills 8 in Bangkok, driver charged with negligence
item.description: A freight train collided with a public bus at a rail crossing in central Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring 32. Thai police have charged the train driver with negligence as investigations continue.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Islamist attack on Nigerian military school kills 17 police trainees
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants attacked a specialized military school in Yobe state, killing at least 17 police officers in training. The attack marks a major escalation in northeast Nigeria's ongoing insurgency.

item.title: 🇺🇸 New York's largest commuter rail system shuts down in first strike since 1994
item.description: The Long Island Rail Road ceased operations as five unions walked off the job after months of stalled contract negotiations. Governor Hochul pleaded with unions to resume talks before Monday's rush hour, as 250,000 daily commuters face disruption.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru electoral body pledges to fix voting flaws before June presidential runoff
item.description: Peru's top electoral authority acknowledged flaws that delayed April's first-round results by a month. The runoff between Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez is scheduled for June 7, following weeks of political uncertainty.

item.title: 🇺🇸 USS Ford Aircraft Carrier Returns Home After 11-Month Iran War Deployment
item.description: The USS Ford aircraft carrier has returned to its home port after an 11-month deployment that supported operations related to the Iran conflict and the capture of Venezuelan leader Maduro. The carrier was one of the longest deployments for a US aircraft carrier in recent years.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Insists It Is Independent After Trump Warning From Beijing Summit
item.description: Taiwan has reaffirmed its independent status after President Trump, fresh from his summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing, warned against a formal declaration of independence by the island. The statement comes amid heightened US-China tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇹 Car Rams Into Pedestrians in Italy, Injuring Eight People
item.description: A driver plowed into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight people, four seriously. Passers-by chased and stopped the attacker. The incident is being investigated for possible terrorist motives.

item.title: 🇳🇬 More Than 50 Schoolchildren Including Toddlers Kidnapped in Nigeria
item.description: Armed gunmen have kidnapped more than 50 schoolchildren, including toddlers, in an attack on a school in Borno state, Nigeria. No group has claimed responsibility for the attack, which has devastated the local community.

item.title: 🚢🇺🇸 US Navy plans Trump-class nuclear battleships to counter China
item.description: The US Navy announced plans to build up to 15 nuclear-powered Trump-class battleships by 2055, dubbed the "Golden Fleet," though analysts question their vulnerability to anti-ship missiles and whether the US has sufficient industrial capacity.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 SBU strikes Moscow defense plants and Crimea airbase in coordinated drone raid
item.description: Ukrainian SBU and armed forces hit the Angstrem semiconductor plant in Zelenograd, the Moscow oil refinery, and military airfields in Crimea in one of the largest drone operations against Russian military-industrial targets.

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 US to indict Raúl Castro over 1996 Brothers to the Rescue shootdown
item.description: The US Justice Department will formally charge former Cuban president Raúl Castro next Wednesday in Miami for the 1996 downing of two civilian aircraft operated by the exile group Brothers to the Rescue, which killed four volunteers.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇵🇰 Iranian president thanks neighbors for blocking territory use against Iran
item.description: Iran President Masoud Pezeshkian praised neighboring countries for preventing "misuse of their territory" against Iran during a meeting with Pakistan interior minister, amid ongoing regional tensions and ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ AIPAC leads record spending to unseat Thomas Massie in House primary
item.description: Pro-Israel groups are pouring unprecedented funds into defeating Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) in what has become the most expensive House primary in history, with AIPAC and aligned super PACs spending millions on attack ads.

item.title: 🦠 WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo-Uganda Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization designated the Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus spreading across Congo and Uganda as a public health emergency of international concern, signaling the need for coordinated global response.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drone Attack Kills Four in Russia, Moscow Hit in Largest Strike in Over a Year
item.description: At least four people were killed in a major Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions, with Moscow facing its largest assault in more than a year, marking an escalation in cross-border strikes.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru Confirms June 7 Presidential Runoff Between Fujimori and Sánchez
item.description: Peruvian electoral authorities officially confirmed the presidential runoff election scheduled for June 7, setting up a contest between Keiko Fujimori and Pedro Sánchez.

item.title: 🇨🇳 US to Weigh Action Against China Over Industrial Overcapacity
item.description: US Trade Ambassador says President Trump will be presented with options if investigations determine Chinese industrial overcapacity harms American interests, as trade tensions persist.

item.title: 🇮🇩🇨🇳 Indonesia Pushes Back Against Chinese Business Complaints Over New Rules
item.description: Chinese businesses issued a blunt warning to President Prabowo that tougher regulations are hurting investor confidence, revealing tensions between Jakarta push for resource control and foreign capital.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Russian Drone Barrage Kills 2, Injures 41 Across Ukraine
item.description: Russia launched 287 attack drones overnight, with Ukraine shooting down 279. Eight drones struck seven locations in the latest escalation of aerial attacks.

item.title: 🇺🇸⛪ White House to Host Mass Prayer Event Promoting Christian Origins of US
item.description: Trump administration officials including Pete Hegseth, Marco Rubio, and Mike Johnson will address a mass prayer meeting in Washington as part of US 250th anniversary celebrations, drawing criticism from those who see it as Christian nationalism.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇳🇱💻 ASML-Tata Sign Semiconductor Deal During Modi Netherlands Visit
item.description: India Prime Minister Modi witnessed the signing of a deal between Dutch tech giant ASML and Tata Electronics to build and scale a semiconductor plant in Gujarat, advancing India chip manufacturing ambitions.

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸🚁 US Assesses Drone Threat From Cuba, Report Says
item.description: Cuba has acquired more than 300 military drones and is discussing plans to use them against the US base at Guantanamo Bay, according to a new assessment raising security concerns.

item.title: 🇦🇪⚠️ Drone Strike Causes Fire at UAE Barakah Nuclear Plant
item.description: A drone strike targeted the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in Abu Dhabi, causing a fire at an electrical generator outside the inner perimeter. No injuries or radiation risks were reported, and the IAEA confirmed normal radiation levels.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China Signals Tariff Cuts After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: China and the US agreed to expand agricultural trade through tariff reductions and address non-tariff barriers following the Beijing summit. The agreement marks a potential de-escalation in trade tensions between the two largest economies.

item.title: 🇰🇷💰 South Korea Pursues All Options to Prevent Samsung Strike
item.description: South Korea's prime minister said the government is considering all measures to prevent a potential Samsung Electronics strike that could cause unimaginable economic damage. Samsung accounts for nearly a quarter of South Korea's exports.
